mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent labor market contraction and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or signal accuracy drops below 52% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in the residential real estate sector, boosting financial and consumer discretionary equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when macro regime shifts to late-cycle stagnation, or 5d returns are negative in >55% of acceleration periods.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a continuous floor for aggregate income and corporate earnings, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth consistently fails to correlate with 20d forward returns (rolling correlation < 0.05) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above the 4% threshold forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, triggering equity market repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets remain flat or positive for >3 months following CPIAUCSL threshold breaches, or the signal shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and rising unemployment indicates a broad-based economic deceleration, compressing corporate profit margins and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-UNRATE divergence metric fails to predict negative 60d returns with >55% accuracy, or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales of existing homes reflect immediate consumer confidence and liquidity, transmitting positive sentiment to equity markets within the next week.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases fail to precede positive 5d S&P 500 returns in >60% of observations, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01.
